title: Conductor Pipeline Closeout Hardening
summary: Plan to implement still-valid recommendations from the email-triage nine-stage pipeline peer review: terminal closeout gate, stale Stage 5 labels, audit corrections, post-doc validation, Bun fallback documentation, and PSParser/mojibake follow-up disposition.
keyOutcomes: Terminal closeout gate defined for Stage 9 and bookkeeping synchronization
Fallback executor agents use correct Stage 5 execution labels
Audit-correction and post-doc validation conventions documented
Bun is not defined recommendation grounded in upstream issue evidence and local fallback protocol
risks: Global agent/skill prompt edits require restart before future sessions see them
Bun issue may be upstream/runtime rather than locally fixable
Email-triage parser cleanup is cross-repo and should not be mixed with OpenCode pipeline prompt edits
deliverables: PSParser/mojibake follow-up track or explicit deferral
validationCriteria: All plan tasks have one authoritative acceptance check
Global file edits are backed up before execution
metadata.json, tracks.md, and tracks-ledger.md are synchronized at closeout
User is told to restart OpenCode after global agent/skill changes
